---
title: "Overlap Report"
author: "Proteomics and Proteostasis Lab"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d/%m/%Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: yes
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
params:
  data:
    label: "Upload PSM file (.tsv):"
    input: file
    value: NULL
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)
```

```{r libraries}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{r load-and-filter}
# Load raw data ----------------------------------------------------------------
raw_psm <- readr::read_delim(
  params$data,
  delim        = "\t",
  escape_double = FALSE,
  trim_ws      = TRUE
)

# Keep only unique PSMs and extract fraction label -----------------------------
psm <- raw_psm %>%
  filter(`Is Unique` == "TRUE") %>%
  mutate(fraction = str_extract(Spectrum, "M\\d+"))

n_fractions <- n_distinct(psm$fraction)
```

```{r build-sets}
# One row per fraction: list of unique peptides / proteins ---------------------
peptide_sets <- psm %>%
  group_by(fraction) %>%
  summarise(items = list(unique(Peptide)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(sub("M", "", fraction)))

protein_sets <- psm %>%
  group_by(fraction) %>%
  summarise(items = list(unique(Protein)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(sub("M", "", fraction)))
```

```{r jaccard-functions}
# Jaccard index between two rows of a set-list data frame ----------------------
jaccard_pair <- function(set_df, i, j) {
  a     <- set_df$items[[i]]
  b     <- set_df$items[[j]]
  inter <- length(intersect(a, b))
  union <- length(union(a, b))
  if (union == 0) return(NA_real_)
  (inter / union) * 100
}

# Full symmetric Jaccard matrix for a set-list data frame ----------------------
build_jaccard_matrix <- function(set_df) {
  n   <- nrow(set_df)
  mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = n,
                dimnames = list(set_df$fraction, set_df$fraction))
  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    for (j in i:n) {
      score      <- jaccard_pair(set_df, i, j)
      mat[i, j]  <- score
      mat[j, i]  <- score
    }
  }
  mat
}

jaccard_peptides <- build_jaccard_matrix(peptide_sets)
jaccard_proteins <- build_jaccard_matrix(protein_sets)
```

```{r heatmap-function}
# Reusable heatmap builder (lower triangle) ------------------------------------
plot_jaccard_heatmap <- function(mat, fill_color, plot_title) {

  fraction_levels <- rownames(mat)

  mat_lower <- mat
  mat_lower[upper.tri(mat_lower)] <- NA

  df_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(mat_lower)) %>%
    rename(frac_x = Var1, frac_y = Var2, jaccard = Freq) %>%
    filter(!is.na(jaccard)) %>%
    mutate(
      frac_x  = factor(frac_x, levels = fraction_levels),
      frac_y  = factor(frac_y, levels = fraction_levels),
      jaccard = round(jaccard, 2)
    )

  ggplot(df_long, aes(frac_x, frac_y, fill = jaccard)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = jaccard), size = 3) +
    scale_fill_gradient(
      low      = "#f5f5f5",
      high     = fill_color,
      na.value = "transparent",
      name     = "Jaccard (%)"
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x  = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      aspect.ratio = 1
    ) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = plot_title)
}
```

---

### Overall summary

```{r summary-table}
summary_stats <- tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "Total PSMs (raw)",
    "PSMs after filtering (unique)",
    "Fractions detected",
    "Unique peptides",
    "Unique proteins"
  ),
  Value = c(
    nrow(raw_psm),
    nrow(psm),
    n_fractions,
    n_distinct(psm$Peptide),
    n_distinct(psm$Protein)
  )
)

summary_stats %>%
  kbl() %>%
  kable_paper("hover", full_width = FALSE, font_size = 17)
```

<br>

```{r barplot, fig.height=5, fig.width=8}
per_fraction <- psm %>%
  group_by(fraction) %>%
  summarise(
    `Unique peptides` = n_distinct(Peptide),
    `Unique proteins` = n_distinct(Protein),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(sub("M", "", fraction))) %>%
  mutate(fraction = factor(fraction, levels = unique(fraction))) %>%
  pivot_longer(-fraction, names_to = "type", values_to = "count")

y_max <- max(per_fraction$count) * 1.12

ggplot(per_fraction, aes(fraction, count, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.75, color = "grey25") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = count),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = -0.15, size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Unique peptides" = "steelblue",
    "Unique proteins" = "darkolivegreen"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, y_max)) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x     = "Fraction",
    y     = "Count",
    fill  = NULL,
    title = "Unique peptides and proteins per fraction"
  )
```

---

### Jaccard index — Peptides

<br>

```{r heatmap-peptides, fig.height=6, fig.width=7}
plot_jaccard_heatmap(
  mat        = jaccard_peptides,
  fill_color = "steelblue",
  plot_title = "Peptide overlap between fractions (Jaccard %)"
)
```

---

### Jaccard index — Proteins

<br>

```{r heatmap-proteins, fig.height=6, fig.width=7}
plot_jaccard_heatmap(
  mat        = jaccard_proteins,
  fill_color = "darkolivegreen",
  plot_title = "Protein overlap between fractions (Jaccard %)"
)
```
